name: How do general contractors usually charge, and what’s a normal rate around here?
headline: How do general contractors usually charge, and what’s a normal rate around here?

mainEntity.name: How do general contractors usually charge, and what’s a normal rate around here?
mainEntity.text: How do general contractors usually charge, and what’s a normal rate around here?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Pacifica and San Mateo County area, many general contractors charge in two main ways: a fixed price for the whole project or cost-plus (their markup added to actual costs). For smaller jobs, some may use hourly rates. Across the Bay Area, hourly rates for general contractors often land somewhere around the $50–$100 per hour range, depending on the contractor’s experience, licensing, and the type of project. But for most remodels, you’ll see a total project price instead of an hourly line item. That total will include labor, basic materials, and the contractor’s overhead and profit. You’ll usually see payments broken into a schedule: a deposit, one or more progress payments, and a final payment after work is complete. In California, it’s a red flag if a contractor asks for a huge deposit. State rules limit how much a licensed contractor can collect up front on home improvement jobs, so you should see something modest at the start and then payments tied to real milestones, like framing completed or cabinets installed. Don’t just compare bottom-line numbers. Read what’s included: demolition, permits, debris hauling, specific finishes, and any allowances for items you’ll choose later. Ask how change orders work and how much mark-up they add to extra work. Surprises often come from unclear scope, not from one bad number.
